title: Ignored `startPool`/`endPool` return values make bounty sweeps silently fail
reason: The low-level calls are part of an intentional brute-force sweep over many candidate parameter sets; ignoring individual failures is expected behavior here, and the candidate does not show a distinct protocol-level harm beyond reduced observability.

title: Counter state is fully mutable by any caller
reason: `Counter.sol` is a trivial sample contract with no stated trust or access-control requirements; public mutability of its lone variable is generic and not a reportable vulnerability in this audit context.
